Here's a list of the main components:

Components PLANNER UTILITY ``

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
1. Install the plugin. See Installation for details.

  2. Ensure the plugin is enabled in the editor:

docs.title: Creating Controller
docs.text:
1. Create a Blueprint Class of type ExampleController:

It should look like this:

  1. For the Planner Component to work, you'll need some actions. Create a Blueprint of type Action:

It should look like this:

  1. Click on Class Defaults. You should see something like this:

This is where you define:

  • Action Name
  • Cost
  • Preconditions
  • Effects

Preconditions determine what must be true (or false) before the action can be executed.

Effects determine what becomes true (or false) after the action has been performed.

Here’s an example of an action where the AI might be dancing, but without any preconditions:

docs.title: Action Execution Override
docs.text:
This is where you define how the action behaves during execution.

  1. Left-click Functions Overridable.
  2. Hover over the overridable function list until you see this:

  1. Click Execute.

You should see an EExitSequenceType input along with an Owner input of type Actor.

If you are using the example controller, Owner will almost certainly be an AIController / ExampleController, so you can safely cast it.

Owner is typed as Actor so users who place their Planner and Reasoner Components directly on the character (instead of the controller) can still use the same execution flow.

Now you can implement the action's functionality.

If you're unsure what this should look like, see the example below:

docs.title: Example Action: Set Material and Teleport AI

docs.title: Member Function(s):
docs.text: EExitSequenceType Execute( Owner);

docs.title: Description :
docs.text:
Defines the possible termination states of a behaviour sequence during execution.

This enum is used to communicate the final or current execution state of a sequence when transitioning out of a node, task, or control flow operation.

docs.title: Values
docs.text: Name Description SUCCESS The sequence completed successfully and met all required conditions. FAILURE The sequence terminated because one or more required conditions failed. INTERRUPTED The sequence was externally interrupted before completion. INVALID The sequence entered an invalid state or produced an undefined result. RUNNING The sequence is still executing and has not yet reached a terminal state. DEFAULT Internal fallback value used when no explicit state has been assigned. Hidden from Blueprint exposure.

docs.title: Structs
docs.text: struct Node

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Inherits from UActorComponent.

Core planner component attached to an AIController. When a goal is assigned, it uses A* search to generate a sequence of actions for the AI to execute.

docs.title: Member Variables
docs.text: UBlackboardComponent* BB_Planner;Allows for Blackboard Integration inside the planner. TArray<UAction*> ToDoStack;Where the planner stores the list of actions, ready to execute by the AI UAction* CurrentAction;Current action being executed by the planner. Runtime-only, not exposed to editor or Blueprint. UAction* LastAction;Debug-only reference to the last executed action. Not Editable or Visible. TArray<TSubclassOf<UAction>> AvailableActions;Stores a list of Action Templates, allows for customization in Editor. Visible and Editable in Editor TArray<UAction*> ActionList;A list of instaned Template actions from Available Actions. FWorldState AgentStateValueAgent State Values, updated during action execution.

docs.title: Member Functions
docs.text:
TArray<UAction*> FilterSatisfyingActions(TArray<UAction*> Actions, const FWorldState DesiredState);

Returns the subset of actions whose effects satisfy at least part of the desired world state. Not Callable in Editor.

TArray<UAction*> PlanGoal(FWorldState CurrentState, FWorldState DesiredState); Returns a subset of actions that when executed achieves the desired World State. TArray<UAction*> BuildPlan(Node* Last); Builds an Array of Actions to form a plan by using Node traversal. void UpdateStack(AActor* OwningActor)

Updates The ToDoStack by executing the top most element from the stack.

FORCEINLINE bool HasPlan() const { return ToDoStack.Num() > 0; } Checks if ToDoStack has elements. If it's greater than 0, then we currently have a plan. FORCEINLINE void AbortPlan() { ToDoStack.Empty(); }Wipes out the elements in the ToDoStack, effectively aborting the plan. e.g. when the Current Action execution is invalid.

docs.title: Delegates
docs.text:
FOnPlanInvalid OnPlanInvalid

Dynamic Multicast Delegate, should be called in code when the plan is invalidated, e.g, a null pointer or an action execution returning EExitSequence::INVALID. Bound function is usually defined in the AIController.

FOnPlanFinished OnPlanFinished

Dynamic Multicast Delegate, should be called in code when the plan is finished, e.g, when the ToDoStack is empty. Bound function is usually defined in the AIController.
